knowledgeBase.content: Tone: professional, direct, no chatty preamble. Audience: enterprise hospitality, cruise lines, J-1 host employers, candidates. Avoid: marketing fluff, unsubstantiated claims, approval/timeline guarantees. Always: ground claims in specific operational evidence. Use the Brand Voice plugin (`brand-voice:enforce-voice`) to apply guidelines to drafts.

Three template families: (1) Sub-agent targeting — sourcing partners in source countries; (2) Recruitment — candidate-facing for J-1 / cruise / hospitality; (3) Partnership — host employer / cruise line BD outreach. Each template has its own messaging pillars and channel rules.

Channels: video (J-1 recruitment, cruise career), email (sequenced nurture per persona), social (LinkedIn primary; selective IG for candidate audience), podcast. Cadence and topic mix to be defined per quarter — current cadence pending upload from Marketing Lead.

Sponsor partners (CIEE, Alliance Abroad, Greenheart): position CTI as the highest-volume placement engine with end-to-end housing. Cruise lines: position GHR as the maritime crewing arm with MLC-compliant pipeline. Differentiation must be ground-truth (numbers + named host employers) — no fabricated claims.

Approximately 154 designated J-1 sponsors and placement agencies operate in the U.S. CTI's competitive position is documented in the BD library. Update at least quarterly. Source: State Department designated sponsor list + private CTI tracker.
Format details, guest selection criteria, and release cadence pending upload from Marketing Lead.
Brand palette and PPT templates archived in POSEIDON\Claude-Workspace. All decks should use the official template — agents that generate decks must apply brand guidelines before delivery.

decisionTrees.trigger: Customer-facing email drafted by an agent

rules.if: Email contains a guarantee, timeline, or pricing
Email is a templated reply matching an approved sequence
rules.then: Draft + queue for Robert; never auto-send
Draft only — Robert sign-off required before publish

rules.if: Claim cites specific numbers or named competitors

escalationRules.trigger: Pricing or timeline statement in marketing copy
